skills.description: Astropy is a Python package for astronomy, providing essential functionality for coordinate transformations, unit and quantity calculations, FITS file operations, cosmological calculations, precise time handling, tabular data manipulation, and astronomical image processing.
trigger_conditions: Working with physical units and quantities
anti_triggers: General programming tasks not related to astronomy

skills.description: Run autonomous research tasks that plan, search, read, and synthesize information into comprehensive reports.
Matplotlib is Python's foundational visualization library for creating static, animated, and interactive plots.
trigger_conditions: Creating any type of plot or chart
anti_triggers: For simple scripts without complex plotting needs
When working with low-level graphics libraries

skills.description: Plotly is a Python library for creating interactive, publication-quality visualizations with over 40 chart types. It supports various APIs like Plotly Express and Graph Objects, and offers extensive customization options.

Scanpy is a scalable Python toolkit for analyzing single-cell RNA-seq data, built on AnnData. It supports complete workflows including quality control, normalization, dimensionality reduction, clustering, marker gene identification, visualization, and trajectory analysis.
trigger_conditions: Performing quality control on scRNA-seq datasets
anti_triggers: Not suitable for single-cell data preprocessing only

skills.description: Seaborn is a Python library for creating publication-quality statistical graphics. It provides a high-level interface for drawing attractive and informative statistical graphics.
trigger_conditions: Need to create visualizations of datasets
anti_triggers: Simple static plots without statistical analysis
Basic line or bar charts only

skills.description: SymPy is a Python library for symbolic mathematics that enables exact computation using mathematical symbols rather than numerical approximations.
